title: Hailey Storage Bed — Twin
summary: A twin bed built on two cubby storage benches with a paneled headboard and footboard — hidden storage under the mattress and a designer look, in one modular system.
description:
This is the foundation of the Hailey storage system: a twin bed that rides on two long cubby benches, so the entire space under the mattress becomes open storage (perfect for fabric bins) instead of dead air. Add the matching towers, hutch, or daybed arms and it grows into a whole bedroom set. On its own it's a sturdy, storage-packed twin.

The benches are simple 1x12 boxes divided into cubbies — keep the middle cubby centered and space the rest to suit your bins — trimmed in 1x2 and backed in 1/4" plywood. Reinforce by fastening the top and bottom trim into the cubby dividers, not just the ends. The signature detail is ripping the 2x4 headboard and footboard legs down to 3" wide (1/4" off each edge) for crisp square faces; that needs a table saw and every panel dimension assumes it, so don't skip it. Taper the footboard legs to match the clearance under the benches.

Two real-world notes the plan makes. Children have accidents, so the mattress needs to breathe — rather than a solid platform, cut a pile of slats to fit the gap between the two benches (measure it, since 1x12 widths vary) and screw them down spaced apart. And the end cubbies are ideal for fabric bins or even a nightstand; if you'd rather hide them, they're easy to fit with doors. Assemble by screwing the headboard and footboard to the benches — the result is extremely sturdy.

tools.note: Optional; finish nails also build the cubby boxes.
Required — the 2x4 legs are ripped to 3", which every panel dimension assumes.

materials.name: 1x12 board, 8 ft
materials.note: Bench tops/bottoms and dividers. Or a plywood sheet ripped to 11-1/2" strips.

cutList.note: Approximate — measure the gap between benches and cut to fit.

steps.title: Build the bench boxes
steps.body: Build a simple box out of 1x12s for each bench, placing the cubbies at any spacing but keeping the middle cubby centered. Build two benches. Finish nails or pocket holes both work.

steps.title: Add legs, trim, and backs
steps.body: Add the 1x2 legs, tops and outsides flush. Trim out the tops and bottoms, securing the trim to the cubby dividers too for strength. Then tack 1/4" plywood to the backs with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue.

steps.title: Build the headboard
steps.body: Rip 1/4" off each side of the 2x4s so they're 3" wide. Build the headboard panel with the ripped legs, headers, and panel trim. Taper the legs slightly if you like the look.

steps.title: Build the footboard
steps.body: Build the footboard the same way, tapering its legs first to match the clearance under the benches. Attach the headers with 2" finish nails and glue.

steps.title: Slat the middle and assemble
steps.body: Assemble the bed by screwing the headboard and footboard to the two benches with 2" screws. Measure the gap between the benches (1x12 widths vary), cut a pile of 1x3 slats to fit, and screw them down spaced apart so the mattress can breathe. Fill, sand, and finish.

images.alt: Twin storage bed built on cubby benches with a paneled headboard
